mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers Hinduism including its Vedic origins, Indus Valley roots, major sects (Vaishnavism), dharma, and modern movements, the primary subject of 823.A-C.
Covers Hindu philosophical systems including Brahman, Vedanta, dharma, karma, and moksha, directly relevant to 823.B.3 on the integral relation of philosophy and religion.
Directly covers Sikhism including Guru Nanak, the ten Gurus, the Punjab, caste, and worship, the specific subject of 823.D.2.
Buddhism arose as a heterodox challenge to Brahmanism, directly relevant to 823.A.4 on challenges to Brahmanism by reformers and ascetic groups.
Covers the Buddha's emergence from the Hindu context including Jainism, four noble truths, and rebirth, relevant to 823.A.4 on the heterodox period.
Gandhi's life and thought embody modern Hindu reform, satyagraha, and ethical nationalism, relevant to 823.A.9 on the modern period and 823.C.2.f on ethical and nationalist movements.
Covers caste, democracy, and religion in contemporary India, relevant to 823.A.10 on Hinduism today and 823.C.3.c on the caste system.
Covers sacrifice, passage rites, and religious ritualization, relevant to 823.C.3.a on sacrifice and worship and 823.C.3.b on sacred times and places.
Covers Tantric elements shared with Hindu Tantrism including Tantra, karma, and guru traditions, relevant to 823.C.2.d on Tantrism and Shaktism.
Covers mystical traditions across Hinduism, Buddhism, and other faiths, relevant to 823.B.4 on Hindu mysticism and its goals, methods, and varieties.
Provides comparative monotheistic context for understanding Hindu theistic and monistic traditions discussed in 823.C.1.a on views about God.
Covers approaches to myth relevant to understanding Hindu mythology's varieties, modes of representation, and themes in 823.B.2.
Provides comparative framework for understanding Hinduism and other religions of India within the broader study of religion.
Provides comparative context for understanding Eastern religious philosophy alongside the Hindu philosophical traditions of 823.B.3.
Covers Pakistan's partition from India and its Islamic identity, relevant to 823.D.2.a.i on Islamic background of Sikhism and 823.D.2.vi on Sikhism in independent Pakistan.
